steps.title: Apply the margin of error to find the plausible range
steps.content:
The sample estimate is 20% with a margin of error of 3%. The plausible range for the true percentage is:
$$20\% - 3\% = 17\% \quad \text{to} \quad 20\% + 3\% = 23\%$$

steps.title: Convert percentage bounds to actual bead counts
steps.content:
With 12,000 total beads:
Lower bound: $17\% \times 12{,}000 = 2{,}040$
Upper bound: $23\% \times 12{,}000 = 2{,}760$
So the plausible range is $2{,}040 \lt r \lt 2{,}760$.

steps.title: Evaluate each choice
steps.content:
• "r is greater than 2,760": 2,760 is the upper bound of the plausible range; values above it are outside the margin of error. ✗
  • "2,040 is less than r, which is less than 2,760": This is exactly the interval 17%–23% of 12,000, matching the estimate plus or minus margin. ✓
  • "360 is less than r, which is less than 2,400": 2,400 corresponds to 20%, the point estimate; values between some lower limit and 2,400 cover only part of the plausible range and exclude the upper half. ✗
  • "r is less than 2,040": 2,040 is the lower bound; values below it are outside the margin of error. ✗
That matches the correct answer — B is correct.
